description: The v0.9.1 fidelity miss — confounded comparison + inference overclaim.

substrate.text: The v1-vs-v3 CogniMap conclusion that CALIC is bad is unsupported because the comparison was confounded — two variables changed simultaneously (predictor type and strategy map). The missing cell is fancy predictor + map.

claim: v3 was worse than v1, so CALIC is bad.

description: Tests passing doesn't imply ship-ready.

substrate.text: A passing test suite is not sufficient evidence for shipping; it doesn't follow that green CI means the change is correct. The conclusion that 'tests pass therefore ship' is unsupported without checking integration, performance, and design review.

claim: The tests pass, so we should ship.

description: Classic correlation/causation overclaim.

substrate.text: The user-engagement spike after the redesign does not prove the redesign caused it; the conclusion is unsupported without a controlled experiment. This is a confounded observation.

claim: Engagement went up after the redesign, so the redesign worked.

description: Skip-tests-for-speed is methodologically unsupported.

substrate.text: Skipping tests as a CI strategy is unsupported as a tradeoff; it confounds regression detection with deploy speed. The conclusion that 'tests are slow therefore skip' doesn't follow.

claim: Tests are slow, so we should skip them.

description: N=1 generalization.

substrate.text: The compression test was N=1 — one task, one image, one substrate. The conclusion that 'aether works for coding governance' is unsupported on this evidence alone; need N>1 across domains.

claim: The compression test passed, so aether works for coding governance.

description: Prohibition + imperative on force push.

substrate.text: Never force push to main; the branch is protected.

description: Prohibition on bypassing pre-commit.

substrate.text: Do not skip pre-commit hooks with --no-verify.
